skill.name: dingtalk-bridge
skill.description: DingTalk group chat bridge for Claude Code. Send markdown/text messages to DingTalk groups, receive @mentions and auto-execute via Claude CLI, run a 24/7 Stream bot. Triggers: dingtalk, send dingtalk, dingtalk bot, dingtalk message, send group message, 钉钉, 发群消息, 钉钉机器人
skill.summary: Bridge Claude Code with DingTalk group chat for bi-directional messaging and automated task execution
skill.author: mguozhen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 208 patterns, mostly false positives from documentation code examples and legitimate API usage. The skill intentionally executes subprocess commands to run the Claude CLI with user prompts, which is properly sandboxed using list arguments (no shell injection). No hardcoded credentials, no prompt injection attempts, no malicious intent detected. All network activity is to official DingTalk API endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution with User Input
medium_findings.description: The stream_bot.py executes the Claude CLI with user-controlled prompt content via subprocess.run. While this is intentional functionality for a chat bot bridge, it allows arbitrary command execution through the Claude CLI. The implementation uses proper list arguments to prevent shell injection, and the --dangerously-skip-permissions flag is used which is documented.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is intentional functionality for a bot bridge - the subprocess execution is the core feature. Properly implemented with list arguments to prevent injection, but users should understand this executes Claude CLI with their prompts.

low_findings.title: macOS LaunchAgent Persistence
low_findings.description: The install.sh script optionally creates a macOS LaunchAgent for 24/7 bot operation. This is a legitimate persistence mechanism for a long-running service, properly documented and requires user consent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: LaunchAgent creation is user-consented, documented, and appropriate for a 24/7 bot service. No malicious persistence pattern detected.

low_findings.title: Credential Storage in Config File
low_findings.description: App credentials are stored in config.json with chmod 600 permissions. This is a standard practice for local development tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proper permission model (chmod 600) used for credential file. Standard practice for CLI tools requiring API credentials.

content.user_title: Connect Claude Code to DingTalk Chat
content.value_statement: Bridge your Claude Code agent with DingTalk group chat for bi-directional messaging. Send notifications and execute commands through @mentions in group conversations.
actual_capabilities: Send rich markdown messages to DingTalk groups from Claude Code
Receive @mentions in DingTalk groups and auto-execute as Claude Code prompts
Run 24/7 stream bot with crash recovery and WebSocket keepalive
Configure via environment variables or JSON file
Support for both webhook and OpenAPI reply paths
limitations: Requires DingTalk Enterprise Internal App credentials (app_key and app_secret)
Only supports one active conversation per configuration file
Stream bot requires continuous process execution (use LaunchAgent or systemd)
Replies truncated to 3000 characters by default

use_cases.title: Development Team Notifications
use_cases.description: Send build status, deployment alerts, or test results from Claude Code directly to your team's DingTalk group

use_cases.title: Interactive Task Automation
use_cases.description: Team members can @mention the bot in DingTalk to trigger Claude Code tasks like code review, documentation generation, or data analysis

use_cases.title: 24/7 Monitoring Bot
use_cases.description: Deploy the stream bot as a persistent service to handle asynchronous requests and execute tasks through Claude Code
use_cases.target_user: Site reliability engineers and system administrators

prompt_templates.title: Send a Simple Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a DingTalk message: The production deployment completed successfully
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick notification to team members about deployment status

prompt_templates.title: Send Markdown with Title
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a DingTalk message with title 'Build Alert' saying: Build #142 failed in the integration tests. 3 tests failing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured notification with title and detailed message

prompt_templates.title: Start the Stream Bot
prompt_templates.prompt: Start the DingTalk stream bot in the background so it can receive @mentions
prompt_templates.scenario: Launch the 24/7 listener for interactive commands

prompt_templates.title: Configure via Environment Variables
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up DingTalk credentials using environment variables DINGTALK_APP_KEY and DINGTALK_APP_SECRET
prompt_templates.scenario: Secure configuration without storing credentials in files

output_examples.input: Send a DingTalk message: Today's build passed all 142 tests
output_examples.output: Message sent successfully to DingTalk group
DingTalk Bridge is configured with app_key, conversation ID saved, and workdir set to /home/user/project

best_practices: Use environment variables for credentials instead of config.json when possible
Start the stream bot using systemd or LaunchAgent for 24/7 operation
Test message sending with the CLI before setting up the stream bot
Keep the Claude CLI updated to ensure compatibility with --dangerously-skip-permissions flag
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode app_key or app_secret in source code or version control
Avoid sending messages longer than 3000 characters without adjusting max_reply configuration
Do not run the stream bot with elevated privileges unless necessary
Never share the config.json file or commit it to version control

faq.question: How do I get DingTalk app credentials?
faq.answer: Go to DingTalk Open Platform (open-dev.dingtalk.com), create an Enterprise Internal App, enable Robot capability, set message mode to Stream, and copy the App Key and App Secret.

faq.question: Can I use this with multiple DingTalk groups?
faq.answer: The default configuration supports one group. For multiple groups, create separate config files with different DINGTALK_CONV_FILE paths or modify the skill to support multiple conversation IDs.

faq.question: Is it safe to execute Claude commands from chat?
faq.answer: The bot uses subprocess.run with list arguments to prevent shell injection. However, Claude CLI will execute with your permissions. Only use this in trusted groups and be aware that anyone who can @mention the bot can trigger Claude execution.

faq.question: What happens if the stream bot crashes?
faq.answer: The bot has exponential backoff retry logic (5s to 60s) and will auto-reconnect. For additional reliability, use systemd or LaunchAgent to auto-restart the process on exit.

faq.question: Does this work on Linux and Windows?
faq.answer: The Python code works on Linux and Windows. The install.sh LaunchAgent setup is macOS-only, but you can use systemd on Linux or create a Windows service equivalent.

faq.question: How do I stop the stream bot?
faq.answer: Press Ctrl+C in the terminal running the bot, or use launchctl stop com.dingtalk-bridge.bot (macOS) or systemctl stop dingtalk-bridge (Linux) if running as a service.
